title: React App Shell
problem: The portfolio needs a runnable React app that reads feature.json files and renders them as a visual timeline — without it, no other features can be built or seen.
analysis: Vite + React scaffold where feature data is imported at build time via import.meta.glob, bundling all feature.json files as static JSON with no server required. The root and all child feature.json files become the dataset the UI renders.

decisions.decision: Vite over Create React App or Next.js
decisions.rationale: Fast dev server, native glob imports for JSON, and outputs a static site with no server runtime required.
Vite natively resolves all matching files at build time into a single import map, requiring no file system API at runtime.
alternativesConsidered: Runtime fetch of JSON files — requires a server

decisions.decision: Dead code removed (byKey, rooted variables in buildTree) during integration sprint
decisions.rationale: The variables were computed but never read, so removing them during the integration sprint was zero-risk.

decisions.decision: types.ts extended with successCriteria and domain to match feature.json schema
decisions.rationale: Both fields were written to feature.json files but invisible to TypeScript — adding them enables type-safe rendering in the card UI.

lineage.spawnReason: The root feature needs a runnable app before any UI work can begin.

implementation: App.tsx renders a tree of FeatureNode components built by buildTree() in features.ts, which uses import.meta.glob('../**/feature.json', { eager: true }) to collect all feature.json files at build time. buildTree() sorts features into a depth-first tree via lineage.parent keys and flattens to an ordered array with depth values for visual indentation.
knownLimitations: No error boundary around App or FeatureCard — malformed feature.json crashes the whole page
The glob pattern has no exclusions — node_modules fixtures would be silently included
No empty-state UI — zero feature.json files renders a blank list with no message
successCriteria: `bun run build` completes without errors and the app renders all feature.json files as an indented tree with correct depth, status badges, and expandable detail sections.
